nameMeanings.name: Moses
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish, Welsh, African American, and African (mainly Nigeria): from the Biblical personal name borne by the Israelite leader who led the Israelites out of Egypt, as related in the Book of Exodus. The Hebrew form of the name is Moshe . It is probably of Egyptian origin, from a short form of an ancient Egyptian personal name such as Rameses or Tutmosis, meaning ‘conceived (by a god)’. However, very early in its history the name acquired a folk etymology, being taken as a derivative of the Hebrew root verb mshh ‘extract or draw (something), e.g. from water’ and was associated with a story of the infant Moses being discovered among the bullrushes by Pharaoh's daughter (Exodus 2: 1-10). As a Welsh surname, it was adopted among Dissenter families in the 18th and 19th centuries. In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, for example Italian Moise , Hungarian Mózes (see Mozes ), Assyrian/Chaldean Moshe , Arabic Musa .
English: variant of Moss , with post-medieval excrescent -s.
English: variant of Moyses, a Cornish personal name derived from Middle English Moises, a vernacular form of Moses (see 1 above).

variants.name: Mose
meanings: Danish: topographic name from mose ‘swamp, bog’.
German (Möse): habitational name from any of several places called Möse.
Italian: from the Biblical name Mose, an equivalent of Moses .

variants.name: Moschel
meanings: Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the Yiddish male personal nameMoshl, a pet form of Moses .

variants.name: Moskowitz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Aron, Emanuel, Ari, Moshe, Chana, Mendel, Mendy, Sholem, Aviva.

Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): patronymic from a Germanized form of the Yiddish personal name Moshke, a pet form of Moshe (see Moses ). The ending -owitz is a Germanized form of the East Slavic patronymic ending -ovich, Polish -owicz.

variants.name: Mosey
meanings: English (Yorkshire): variant of Moysey, a personal name derived from a Middle English vernacular form of the Biblical name Moses .

variants.name: Mosko
meanings: Polish (Mośko), Slovak (Moško), Ukrainian, and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): from a Slavic pet form of the personal name Moses .
American shortened and altered form of Greek Moschos or of any of various derivatives of this name, such as Moschogiannis (see Moskos ).

variants.name: Mossberg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Sven, Bjorn, Ludvig.  German Bernhard, Kurt.  Jewish Chaya, Herschel, Hyman.
Swedish: ornamental name composed of the elements mosse ‘peat bog’ + berg ‘mountain, hill’.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial extension of Moses , with German Berg ‘mountain, hill’.

variants.name: Moise
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Fritz, Serge, Gessy, Jacques, Monique, Odette, Pierre, Roch, Agathe, Benoit, Bonard, Colette, Collette.
French and West Indian (mainly Haiti) (Moïse); Romanian and Jewish: from the personal name French Moïse, Romanian and Jewish Moise, equivalents of Biblical Moses .
Italian (also Moisé): from a variant of the personal name Mosè, an equivalent of Biblical Moses .

variants.name: Moises
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Ernesto, Raymundo, Adela, Adolfo, Adriana, Alfonso, Alvarez, Ana, Aurelio, Carlos, Clarita, Domingo.  French Michel, Pierre.
Spanish, Portuguese, and Jewish (Sephardic) (Moisés): from the personal name Moisés, an equivalent of Moses .

variants.name: Moisan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Jean Louis, Ludger, Maxime, Pierre, Rolande.
Breton: from the Old French oblique case of the personal name Moïse, a French form of Moses .

variants.name: Moshe
meanings: Jewish (Israeli) and Assyrian/Chaldean: from the personal name Moshe, the Hebrew and Syriac (Aramaic) form of Moses . As a Jewish name it can also be a Hebraicized form of some original Ashkenazic surname.

nameMeanings.name: Edyth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of   Edith .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: Edyth Moses was born in Bampton, Oxfordshire, England. She married William de Windsor III about 1188, in England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons.
